preview_rows.description: Key IDs for bushel claims, where multiple inferential tests were evaluated within a single study.
Which bushel claims use non-significant evidence.
Set of sample sizes calculated via the SER method.
Set of sample sizes calculated via traditional methods.
Claims extracted from original studies.
The unique identifier for each paper in the project
The internal unique identifiers for bushel claims
The unique identifier for each paper in the project
Claim indicator variable drawn from stitched_claims.
The human coded statistical evidence drawn from stitched_claims
Indicates whether the evidence for the bushel claim is non-significant.
Unique identifier for the specific claim that the replication attempt was testing.
Sample size needed to meet threshold power, defined as 50% power to detect 100% of the original effect.
Sample size needed to meet the stage 1 power, defined as 90% power to detect 75% of the original effect.
Sample size needed to meet the stage 2 power, defined as 90% power to detect 50% of the original effect.
Unique identifier for the specific claim that the replication attempt was testing.
Sample size needed to meet threshold power, defined as 50% power to detect 100% of the original effect.
Sample size needed to meet the stage 1 power, defined as 90% power to detect 75% of the original effect.
Sample size needed to meet the stage 2 power, defined as 90% power to detect 50% of the original effect.
Unique identifier for the specific claim that the replication attempt was testing.
Indicates if a bushel claim has a functionally equivalent single trace claim.
Raw name of the methodology/analysis/technique (e.g. analysis_of_variance_anova)
Always ‘method’ in this data
Score quantifying how commonly this method appears across abstracts (0 to 100).
Constant (173): Total abstracts sampled.
Standard error (SE) of the llm_score estimate
Unique identifier for each research question
Number of abstracts associated with each research question
Text of the research question, describing its focus or thematic area
Raw name of the paradigm/theory (e.g., behavioral_economics_cognitive_decision_making)
Always “theory” in this dataset
Score indicating how commonly this theory/theme appears in abstracts (0 to 100)
Total abstracts sampled (constant: 173)
Standard error of the llm_score estimate
The unique identifier for each paper in the project

Unique claim identifier, formed by combining paper_id with a claim id (since single-trace claims are not otherwise uniquely identified). Claims that include the phase 'single-trace' are single-trace, and otherwise are bushel claims.
The type of analysis used
The number of parameters in the model

A bare-minimum sample size value for a data-analytic replication attempt; its value is 50% power to detect 100% of the original effect size at the 5% significance level in a two-sided test. This value is used to classify data analytic replications when limited applicable novel data is available (e.g. waves of the same study not used in the original paper).
